formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
[
  {
    "name": "Fick's Law of Diffusion",
    "formula": "J = -DA \* (C1 - C2)/x",
    "variables": "J: rate of diffusion, D: diffusion coefficient, A: surface area, C1 and C2: concentrations of the substance on either side, x: thickness of the membrane",
    "units": "SI units: J = mol/s, D = m^2/s, A = m^2, C1 and C2 = mol/m^3, x = m",
    "notes": "This law describes the movement of substances across a membrane."
  },
  {
    "name": "Gibbs-Donnan Equilibrium",
    "formula": "P = R \* T \* ln(C1/C2)",
    "variables": "P: osmotic pressure, R: gas constant, T: temperature, C1 and C2: concentrations of the solutes",
    "units": "SI units: P = Pa, R = J/mol/K, T = K, C1 and C2 = mol/m^3",
    "notes": "This law describes the equilibrium between two solutions separated by a semipermeable membrane."
  },
  {
    "name": "Law of Conservation of Mass",
    "formula": "Mass in = Mass out",
    "variables": "Mass in: mass of substances entering the system, Mass out: mass of substances leaving the system",
    "units": "SI units: kg",
    "notes": "This law describes the principle that mass is neither created nor destroyed in a chemical reaction."
  },
  {
    "name": "First Law of Thermodynamics",
    "formula": "ΔE = Q - W",
    "variables": "ΔE: change in energy, Q: heat energy, W: work done",
    "units": "SI units: ΔE = J, Q = J, W = J",
    "notes": "This law describes the relationship between energy and work done in a system."
  },
  {
    "name": "Second Law of Thermodynamics",
    "formula": "ΔS = ΔQ / T",
    "variables": "ΔS: change in entropy, ΔQ: heat energy, T: temperature",
    "units": "SI units: ΔS = J/K, ΔQ = J, T = K",
    "notes": "This law describes the relationship between entropy and heat energy in a system."
  },
  {
    "name": "Michaelis-Menten Kinetics",
    "formula": "Vmax = Vm \* [S]",
    "variables": "Vmax: maximum rate of reaction, Vm: maximum velocity, [S]: concentration of substrate",
    "units": "SI units: Vmax = mol/s, Vm = mol/s, [S] = mol/m^3",
    "notes": "This law describes the relationship between the rate of enzyme-catalyzed reaction and substrate concentration."
  }
]
